behavior_summary: The module is a rising-edge-triggered up/down counter with registered state `value` and combinational next-state output `value_next`. On each cycle, synchronous active-high `rst` loads `value` with `initial_value`. Active-high `reinit` also synchronously loads `initial_value` and overrides any increment/decrement request in that cycle. Otherwise, the next state is computed from the current `value`: add `incr` if `incr_valid` is asserted, subtract `decr` if `decr_valid` is asserted, apply both as a net signed change `(incr - decr)` if both valids are high, and hold if neither is high. The counter state is defined over the inclusive range 0..10 and wraps modulo 11 on overflow or underflow. `value` is the registered current state after the last rising edge; `value_next` is the combinational prediction of the state that will be loaded on the next rising edge under the present inputs.
edge_cases: If `rst` is high on a rising edge, `value` is synchronously loaded with `initial_value`.
If `reinit` is high on a rising edge, `value` is synchronously loaded with `initial_value` and any concurrent increment/decrement request is ignored.
If both `incr_valid` and `decr_valid` are high, the applied arithmetic is the net change `(incr - decr)` in the same cycle.
If neither `incr_valid` nor `decr_valid` is high, the counter holds its current state.
Overflow wraps within the 0..10 range modulo 11, e.g. 9 + 3 -> 1.
Underflow wraps within the 0..10 range modulo 11, e.g. 1 - 3 -> 9.
Zero net change must leave the state unchanged, including cases where both valids are high and `incr == decr`.
The spec states `incr` and `decr` have maximum value 3, so values outside 0..3 should not be assumed legal stimulus.

The spec does not explicitly say whether `value_next` reflects `rst`, but since it represents the next state at the next rising edge, including reset in the next-state calculation is the safest inference.
state_transitions: ANY_STATE -> `initial_value` when `rst` is asserted at a rising edge.
ANY_STATE -> `initial_value` when `reinit` is asserted at a rising edge and `rst` is not taking precedence.
STATE v -> ((v + incr) mod 11) when `incr_valid=1` and `decr_valid=0`.
STATE v -> ((v - decr) mod 11) when `incr_valid=0` and `decr_valid=1`.
STATE v -> ((v + incr - decr) mod 11) when `incr_valid=1` and `decr_valid=1`.
priorities: Within arithmetic update, increment and decrement are combined as one net operation; neither has priority over the other when both valids are high.
mutation_risks: Implementing saturation instead of wrap-around modulo 11.
Using modulo 16 or raw 4-bit wrap instead of modulo 11 over the legal state range 0..10.
Giving increment or decrement priority when both valids are high instead of applying the net change.
Ignoring `reinit` priority and still applying incr/decr in the same cycle.
Treating `rst` as asynchronous instead of synchronous.
Loading zero on reset/reinit instead of loading `initial_value`.
Computing underflow incorrectly for negative results.
Driving `value_next` from stale state or making it registered instead of combinational.
Making `value` update with wrong latency relative to `value_next`.
Mishandling zero-change cases such as `incr_valid=decr_valid=1` with equal operands.
Ignoring one valid signal when both are asserted.
executable_reference: Maintain a software model state `model_value` in the range 0..10.
At all times, compute `expected_value_next` from current `model_value` and current inputs.
Else compute `delta = (incr_valid ? incr : 0) - (decr_valid ? decr : 0)` and set `expected_value_next = ((model_value + delta) % 11 + 11) % 11`.
Check combinationally that `value_next == expected_value_next` after inputs settle.
On each rising edge of `clk`, update `model_value = expected_value_next` and then check that registered output `value` matches `model_value` after the edge.
Restrict strict checking to legal operand values `incr, decr` in 0..3 unless the spec is extended.
guaranteed_properties: `value` changes only on the rising edge of `clk`.
`rst` is synchronous, active high, and loads `initial_value` into `value`.
`reinit` is synchronous, active high, and loads `initial_value` into `value`.
When `reinit` is asserted, concurrent increment/decrement requests are ignored.
When both `incr_valid` and `decr_valid` are asserted, the net change `(incr - decr)` is applied.
When neither valid is asserted, the counter holds its current value.
The externally intended counting range is 0 through 10 inclusive.
Overflow and underflow wrap around within that range.
`value_next` is a combinational indication of the next state for the next rising edge.
ambiguous_properties: Whether `value_next` must explicitly reflect `rst` in addition to `reinit` is not stated in the Outputs section, though it is a strong next-state inference.
The behavior if `initial_value` is outside the legal 0..10 range is unspecified.
The exact timing tolerance for observing combinational `value_next` after input changes is unspecified beyond being combinational.
The exact power-up state before any clock edge or reset is unspecified.
The spec does not define behavior for illegal `incr`/`decr` values above 3 even if the bus width allows them.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume any asynchronous reset behavior.
Do not assume `value` changes in the same cycle without a rising clock edge.
Do not assume saturation at 0 or 10; the spec requires wrap-around.
Do not assume 4-bit modulo-16 behavior; the required wrap domain is modulo 11 over 0..10.
Do not assume a specific behavior for out-of-range `initial_value` unless the spec or reference RTL guarantees it.
Do not assume illegal `incr`/`decr` encodings above 3 must be handled in any particular way.
Do not assume internal implementation details such as add-first or subtract-first matter when both valids are asserted; only the final net result is guaranteed.
Do not assert exact delta-cycle ordering between `value` and `value_next` beyond `value_next` being combinational and `value` being registered.
